analyst_observations: The restore-snapping test shows the skill's primary value: 15K tokens vs 89K tokens (6x reduction), 32s vs 145s (4.5x faster). The skill prevents Claude from re-reading source files.

The memorize-snapping test shows similar token usage (84K vs 83K) but the with-skill version took longer (310s vs 203s). The skill adds structural overhead during memorize but produces a more consistent output format with a date stamp.

The restore-nonexistent test reveals a key behavioral difference: with the skill, Claude cleanly reports 'not found' and suggests the memorize command (3/3 pass). Without it, Claude goes exploring the codebase looking for payment-related files (1/3 pass).
The 'last-updated date' assertion is non-discriminating for restore tests but discriminates on memorize: the skill template includes it, baseline doesn't.
Overall token savings are 39% across all tests, but the real value shows in restore mode where it's 83% fewer tokens.
